title: thymus serpyllum var. polytrichus
intro: Thymus serpyllum var. polytrichus, also known as creeping thyme, is a low-growing, spreading herb native to Europe and parts of Asia, characterized by its tiny leaves and pink to purple flowers. The plant has been used for centuries in herbal medicine due to its antimicrobial, antiseptic, and anti-inflammatory properties, which make it effective in treating respiratory issues, such as coughs and colds. The health benefits of creeping thyme include its ability to soothe sore throats, reduce stress and anxiety, and even exhibit antioxidant properties that can protect against cell damage. Creeping thyme can be prepared in various ways, including as a tea, oil, or tincture, making it a versatile and accessible herbal remedy.
